hook: The best teasing usually carries warmth that makes the other person feel invited rather than examined.
body: Play works when the underlying frame is goodwill, not scoring points.

hook: Some men try to sound sharp before they have earned enough comfort for sharpness to feel safe.
body: Keep the edge light enough that the person still feels with you, not up against you.

hook: Keep the edge light enough that the person still feels with you, not up against you.
body: A teasing callback after good rapport can be charming, while the same line too early can land as social overreach.

body: Before teasing, check whether the interaction already has enough warmth to carry it.
